data.text: I’m reading the existing design docs and the ddx documentation rules first so I can place the new TD in the right format and verify the exact acceptance criteria before editing anything.
The first shell launch path failed, so I’m switching to a plain bash session and continuing the repository scan from there.
I’m checking whether the container’s shell lives somewhere other than `/bin/*`; once I can get a working command runner, I’ll inspect the doc tree and the bead metadata.
I’m brute-forcing a few common shell locations because the runner is failing before it can execute even `pwd`; if one of these exists, I can finish the bead normally.

The blocker is now clear: the command runner cannot start any shell in this environment, so I can’t inspect or edit the repository safely. I’m recording that as execution evidence instead of fabricating a partial doc.

I couldn’t complete the bead because the local command runner cannot start any shell binary in this environment, so I couldn’t inspect the repo or author the TD safely.

I wrote the required execution evidence here: [no_changes_rationale.txt](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-23cbcb4b-20260507T014221-1ec64390/.ddx/executions/20260507T014221-1ec64390/no_changes_rationale.txt)
